DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT ELIZABETH PHILLIPS' MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. WHILE INFORMAL DISCOVERY IS ALLOWED, FAILURE TO COMPLY DOES NOT RESULT IN COURT INTERVENTION. (SEE PULLIN V. SUPERIOR COURT (2000) 81 CAL.APP.4TH 1161.) ALSO NO COURTESY COPY OF THE MOTION TO COMPEL WAS DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 301 AS REQUIRED BY LOCAL RULE 2.7(B). ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS). JUDGE PRO TEM: ROBERT KANE, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Jun-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Elizabeth Phillips, esq. (415-830-1626) Defendant in propria persona.
No other appearances.

Discussed. Defendant did not comply with tentative ruling rule. The Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ELIZABETH PHILLIPS' Motion To Compel Production Of Documents

Denied without prejudice. While informal discovery is allowed, failure to comply does not result in court intervention. (See Pullin v. Superior Court (2000) 81 Cal.App.4th 1161.) Also no courtesy copy of the motion to compel was delivered to Department 301 as required by Local Rule 2.7(B). Order is signed. (See Scanned Order for Details).

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
